pull.title: ci: Add workaround for ASLR bug in sanitizers
pull.body:
Fixes #1506.

This also adds the sanitizer env variables to our debug output as suggested in the same issue.

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

events.body: The shell problem is resolved now, but the underlying issue seems unchanged.

Yes, it is because of `sysctl: permission denied on key "vm.mmap_rnd_bits", ignoring`

See https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/actions/runs/8347067930/job/22845907812?pr=1507#step:3:587

events.message:
ci: Add workaround for ASLR bug in sanitizers

Fixes #1506.
ci: Add sanitizer env variables to debug output

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -36,6 +36,9 @@ runs:
         load: true
         cache-from: type=gha
 
+    - # Workaround for https://github.com/google/sanitizers/issues/1614 .
+      run: sysctl -w vm.mmap_rnd_bits=28
comments.body:
```

```
